danger_signals.meaning: Player must solve puzzle before timer expires; running out of time locks player out of turn
Landing on these squares causes player token to fall back to level 3, losing board position progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Partial picture gradually revealed during drawing phase
danger_signals.meaning: Player must guess before full picture appears; incomplete information creates puzzle difficulty and time pressure

safety_signals.visual_form: Connected dot pattern shown before full drawing
safety_signals.meaning: Visual aid that reduces puzzle difficulty and provides player confidence on Easy/Medium modes
Contextual hint that guides player toward correct answer on lower difficulty settings
Structural clue about answer length, reducing guessing uncertainty on easier difficulties
Opportunity for dynamic board traversal rather than linear progression; reduces monotony

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pattern dots shown, category label visible, letter blanks displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game provides visual scaffolding; puzzle-solving aids reduce guessing time

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No dots, no category label, no letter blanks; full drawing revealed gradually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must rely on intuition and cultural knowledge; puzzle difficulty increases dramatically

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Board layout changes; Feature Squares appear on level 3; Drop-Out Squares appear on level 4
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player token moves upward on board; increased risk of falling backward on later levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Die countdown active, drawing being revealed, player can buzz in
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player attempts to identify image quickly; first to buzz gains solving opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Die and drawing freeze after two wrong answers or timeout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot participate until next puzzle cycle; other players gain opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Board board shows four color zones; player token advances in color-specific path
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Completing all four colors advances player to next level; colors track progress visually

environmental_storytelling.example: Game board divided into color zones (blue, green, pink, yellow) that correspond to puzzle categories; player movement through colored spaces reinforces category association
Levels 1-2 are simple linear paths; level 3 introduces Feature Squares for dynamic movement; level 4 adds Drop-Out Squares creating risk/reward tension
Four unique game pieces (high-heeled shoes, bugle, ice cream cone, teddy bear) create distinct player visual identity and allow easy tracking of multiple players on shared board
Visual aids (dots, labels, blanks) systematically disappear as difficulty increases, making player progression toward mastery visually apparent

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves forward on board by number shown on die; collects color credit toward level completion
Flies around board until button pressed; puzzle played in color where token lands
Falls back to corresponding position on level 3; previously collected level 4 colors retained
Gradually reveals partial image while die counts down; freezes when player buzzes in

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Puzzle unsolved or player locked out
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Resumes countdown and continues revealing; allows other players to buzz in
Buzzes in during countdown; attempts to solve puzzles based on difficulty setting

notes: Anticipation is a board game adaptation with minimal world-state complexity. Visual communication is primarily through token positioning, color-coded zones, and difficulty-level scaffolding rather than environmental or political signaling. The game uses color identity (pink, blue, green, yellow) to distinguish players and categories, creating a simple competitive hierarchy. Danger signals are mechanical (time pressure, board hazards) rather than thematic. The game lacks faction conflict, enemy NPCs, or environmental storytelling beyond board design. Visual manifestation is purely systemic and functional.
